what_it_does: Records the current build state and resume point for the operator-surface v2 loop, listing completed foundation work, the next prioritized beats, parallel-session context, what survives or dies across a WSL restart, and post-restart re-entry steps.
observations: Loop-prep is complete and the three docs in this directory are committed.
Foundation beat F3 is done, verified, committed as 790739b, with acceptance test tests/board_open_fields_acceptance.py passing 7/7.
Next autonomous foundation beats in order are F1, F4, F5, then F2 only after live-injection transport migration.
A parallel session is working on identity fusion at HEAD d03fb44, related to B4/S6b, and the file warns not to clobber it.
What survives a WSL restart includes git history, uncommitted working-tree files, the board store, memory, and build-prep docs.
Ephemeral ports listed as dying are :8421, :8783, :8791, and :8782; :8783 is the retired standalone prototype.
Post-restart checklist is: confirm bridge at :8770 is warm, re-read git log and the operator-surface board, then resume at F1.
standouts: Resume-here state after a WSL restart.
Explicit warning about parallel-session divergence at HEAD d03fb44.
Detailed mortality list of ports/services that do not survive restart.
F3 acceptance test reference and commit hash.
conventions: Markdown header hierarchy with a metadata block at the top.
References commit hashes, board item IDs, and external files such as COMPLETION_CRITERIA.md.
Uses bold labels and ordered priority lists for next actions.
Includes an operational checklist for post-restart re-entry.
concerns: About 180 uncommitted working-tree files from multiple sessions create collision risk.
The bridge at :8770 is required but no start command is given here.
Parallel work at d03fb44 may touch the same files as upcoming F1/F4 beats.
The note about port :8782 ('another python (not mine)') is vague.
notes: This is the loop's post-restart anchor: read it to know what's done, what's next, and what not to touch.
questions: What are the exact definitions and acceptance criteria for beats F1, F4, F5, F2, B1-B4, P1-P2, and S0-S15?
How is the bridge at :8770 started or confirmed warm?
What is the scope of the identity-fusion work at HEAD d03fb44 and how should it fuse with F1/F4?
What do the three loop-prep docs and COMPLETION_CRITERIA.md contain?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a restart-resume snapshot of the operator-surface build, exactly as its title and opening state.
apparent_themes: Separation of durable versus ephemeral runtime state
intention_signals: Provide a self-contained re-entry point so a restarted session can safely resume autonomous foundation work without losing context or colliding with a parallel session.
novelty: It treats the build directory as a resumable loop, explicitly listing port mortality and parallel-session divergence warnings rather than only code status.
contribution: A durable, human-readable checkpoint that records the current build priority order, completed verification, and collision warnings, which a fused system would keep as a session-resumption artifact.
summary_for_embedding: STATE.md is a resumable build-state checkpoint for the operator-surface v2 loop that records completed foundation work, the next prioritized beats, parallel-session divergence warnings, what persists after a WSL restart, and post-restart re-entry steps.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to let a restarted session safely pick up the operator-surface build loop without losing context or clobbering parallel work.
